description: Width-1 read at the WGPIPE base address fails closed as unsupported (outcome 3: no read value model).
Width-2 read at the WGPIPE base address fails closed as unsupported.
Width-4 read at the WGPIPE base address fails closed as unsupported.
Width-8 read fails closed as unsupported for the width itself (independent of the read policy).
Misalignment is checked before the read-policy gate: an odd-offset width-2 read fails closed as misaligned, not unsupported.
Misalignment before read-policy gate: offset+2 width-4 read fails closed as misaligned.
Neighboring address just before the region (base-4): the read-policy gate is checked before the range gate, so this is still unsupported, not out-of-range.
note: read_policy=unsupported short-circuits before validate_access's range check; see device_model.GxFifoStreamDevice.validate_access order.

description: Neighboring address just after the region (base+span): same read-policy-before-range ordering, still unsupported, not out-of-range.
note: read_policy=unsupported short-circuits before validate_access's range check; see device_model.GxFifoStreamDevice.validate_access order.

description: Repeated reads at the same address never advance the cursor and never record a read event (no RAW/WAR ordering to preserve under outcome 3).
note: RAW/WAR ordering relative to prior writes is undefined for equivalence purposes under outcome 3; this corpus makes no claim about read values, only that reads never advance state or record events.

description: Width-1 read at the last legal byte of the region still fails closed as unsupported.
Width-4 read mid-region fails closed as unsupported.
A read/write interleave: reads never advance the write cursor and never observe prior writes (outcome 3 makes no read-after-write claim).
note: reordered-read sharing is not applicable under outcome 3 (there is no read value to share); documented here rather than asserted numerically.
